papers.title: Phase 1 Executive Summary: Project Hamilton
papers.organization: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston & MIT Digital Currency Initiative
papers.description: Groundbreaking research exploring CBDC architecture. Demonstrates that a high-performance, resilient, and secure CBDC system is technically feasible. A foundational reference for digital currency infrastructure.

papers.title: The Rise of Digital Money
papers.description: Seminal IMF paper analyzing the emergence of digital money—e-money, stablecoins, and CBDC—and their implications for monetary policy, financial stability, and banking systems.

papers.title: Stablecoins: Risks, Potential and Regulation
papers.description: BIS Working Paper examining the risks associated with global stablecoins, their potential benefits for cross-border payments, and the regulatory implications for financial stability.

papers.title: Regulation, Supervision and Oversight of Global Stablecoin Arrangements
papers.description: FSB final report setting out high-level recommendations for the regulation and oversight of global stablecoin arrangements, covering governance, legal certainty, and risk management.

papers.title: CBDC: Central Bank Digital Currencies — Foundational Principles and Core Features
papers.description: Report from BIS and seven central banks establishing foundational principles and core features for any CBDC, emphasizing coexistence with existing monetary systems.

papers.title: Digital Money Across Borders: Macro-Financial Implications
papers.description: Analyzes how the proliferation of digital money could alter cross-border capital flows, affect exchange rates, and reshape monetary sovereignty—especially in emerging economies.

papers.title: Money and Payments: The U.S. Dollar in the Digital Age
papers.organization: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
papers.description: The Federal Reserve's discussion paper exploring the potential benefits and risks of a U.S. CBDC, focusing on preserving the dollar's role in digital payments.

papers.title: Ready, Steady, Go? — Third BIS Survey on Central Bank Digital Currency
papers.description: Survey of 65 central banks on CBDC work. Finds that over 80% are engaged in CBDC research, with retail CBDC development accelerating across both advanced and emerging economies.

papers.title: Improving Cross-Border Payments: Building Blocks of a Global Roadmap
papers.description: Identifies the core frictions in cross-border payments—cost, speed, access, transparency—and outlines building blocks for improvement, directly informing modern payment system design.

papers.title: G20 Roadmap for Enhancing Cross-Border Payments
papers.description: The G20's three-stage roadmap to reduce costs and increase speed of cross-border payments, aiming for a world where payments are faster, cheaper, more transparent, and more inclusive.

papers.title: Central Bank Digital Currencies for Cross-Border Payments
papers.organization: Bank for International Settlements / Committee on Payments and Market Infrastructures
papers.description: Report on how CBDC could improve cross-border payments by reducing frictions in the correspondent banking system, with analysis of multi-CBDC arrangements.

papers.title: Recommendations of the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures
papers.organization: Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD)
papers.description: Foundational framework for climate-related financial risk disclosure. Establishes governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics standards adopted globally—a core reference for ESG-linked investment analysis.

papers.title: Transforming Our World: The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development
papers.description: The UN's 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) — the universal framework for ending poverty, protecting the planet, and ensuring prosperity. The philosophical foundation for ESG-linked stablecoin redistribution.

papers.title: A Call for Action: Climate Change as a Source of Financial Risk
papers.organization: Network for Greening the Financial System (NGFS)
papers.description: NGFS foundational report calling on central banks and supervisors to incorporate climate risk into financial stability monitoring — a key catalyst for ESG-weighted financial instruments.

papers.title: Climate Change and Financial Stability
papers.description: IMF analysis of how climate change poses risks to macroeconomic and financial stability, including transition risks and physical risks, and the role of financial sector policy.

papers.title: Mobilizing Private Finance for Nature
papers.description: World Bank report on scaling private investment in nature-based solutions, biodiversity, and natural capital — directly relevant to scaling private investment for environmental outcomes.

papers.title: EU Taxonomy Regulation — Establishing a Framework to Facilitate Sustainable Investment
papers.description: The EU's science-based classification system defining which economic activities are environmentally sustainable. A key reference for ESG scoring methodology and compliance frameworks.

papers.title: IOSCO Sustainable Finance and the Role of Securities Regulators
papers.description: IOSCO's analysis of how securities regulators can support sustainable finance through ESG disclosure, green bonds, and product standards — applicable to tokenized ESG instruments.

papers.title: Principles for Responsible Investment
papers.organization: United Nations Principles for Responsible Investment (UN PRI)
papers.description: Six principles adopted by institutional investors committing to incorporate ESG factors into investment decision-making — the global standard for responsible capital allocation.

papers.title: Financial Stability Implications from FinTech
papers.description: FSB's first major analysis of fintech's implications for financial stability, covering technology innovation, regulatory perimeters, and the evolution of financial intermediation.

papers.title: FinTech and Financial Services: Initial Considerations
papers.description: IMF staff discussion note examining fintech's potential to improve financial access, efficiency, and stability — and the associated risks requiring regulatory attention.

papers.title: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA)
papers.description: The EU's comprehensive regulatory framework for crypto-assets, including stablecoins (asset-referenced tokens and e-money tokens). Sets reserve, redemption, and disclosure requirements.

papers.title: OCC Interpretive Letter #1179 — Bank Cryptocurrency Activities
papers.organization: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
papers.description: OCC letter clarifying that national banks may engage in cryptocurrency activities including cryptocurrency custody and participation in blockchain networks — a key regulatory green light for institutional DeFi.

papers.title: Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) and Blockchain
papers.description: World Bank primer on DLT and blockchain for financial sector applications, including payments, settlement, identity, and land registries — a reference for policymakers evaluating blockchain deployment.

papers.title: Prospect Theory: An Analysis of Decision under Risk
papers.description: Nobel Prize-winning paper establishing prospect theory — the foundation of behavioral economics. Shows that people evaluate outcomes relative to reference points and are loss-averse, critical for understanding stablecoin adoption resistance.

papers.title: World Development Report 2015: Mind, Society, and Behavior
papers.description: World Bank's comprehensive report applying behavioral science to development policy. Demonstrates how cognitive biases, social norms, and mental models shape economic decisions — foundational for understanding investor behavior.

papers.title: Behavioural Economics: Implications for Regulatory Strategy
papers.description: IMF technical note examining how behavioral insights can improve financial regulation, supervision, and policy design — particularly relevant to ESG compliance and behavioral investment strategies.

papers.title: Nudge: Improving Decisions About Health, Wealth, and Happiness
papers.description: Seminal work on libertarian paternalism and choice architecture — how default options and framing can guide better decisions without restricting freedom. Core reference for behavioral finance and incentive design.

papers.title: Gender-Smart Investment in MSME Finance
papers.description: IFC analysis of the business case for gender-smart financial product design, showing that women-owned businesses are creditworthy, underserved, and represent a $1.7 trillion financing gap.

papers.title: Just Good Investing: Why Gender Lens Investing Makes Financial Sense
papers.description: Research demonstrating the financial performance case for gender lens investing — companies with gender-diverse leadership show higher returns, lower risk, and better ESG outcomes.

papers.title: Women's Financial Inclusion — Policy Research Working Paper
papers.description: World Bank research on the barriers to women's financial inclusion globally, including legal discrimination, social norms, and product design, and policy interventions to close the gap.

papers.title: Leaving No One Behind: A Call to Action for Gender Equality
papers.description: UN Women's policy framework for accelerating progress toward gender equality as central to achieving the 2030 SDGs, with specific focus on economic empowerment and financial systems.

papers.title: Greening the Financial System: The New Frontier
papers.organization: Network for Greening the Financial System (NGFS)
papers.description: NGFS progress report outlining the expansion of sustainable finance tools — including green bonds, climate stress testing, and biodiversity risk — essential context for ESG oracle design.

papers.title: Fintech: Financial Inclusion or Exclusion?
papers.description: IMF working paper examining whether fintech broadens financial access or creates new divides, with implications for digital payment design and regulatory inclusivity principles.

papers.title: Remittance Prices Worldwide — Quarterly Data
papers.description: World Bank's ongoing monitoring of remittance costs globally. Average cost remains ~6.3% — far above the SDG target of 3%. Foundational data for understanding remittance market inefficiencies.

papers.title: The Global Findex Database 2021: Financial Inclusion, Digital Payments, and Resilience
papers.description: Comprehensive survey of financial inclusion across 123 economies. Shows digital payment adoption accelerated during COVID-19, with 1.4 billion adults still unbanked — the market opportunity for programmable money.

papers.title: Stablecoins and the Future of Money
papers.description: Analysis of stablecoins as programmable money primitives — exploring their role in payments, DeFi, and enterprise treasury, with a framework for evaluating design trade-offs.

papers.title: Crypto-Assets: Implications for Financial Stability, Monetary Policy, and Payments and Market Infrastructures
papers.description: ECB Occasional Paper analyzing crypto-assets across three dimensions: financial stability, monetary policy effectiveness, and market infrastructure — the EU regulatory foundation for digital asset policy.

papers.title: Decentralized Finance (DeFi): Risks, Regulation, and the Road Ahead
papers.description: FSB's first comprehensive assessment of DeFi, identifying interconnections with traditional finance, structural vulnerabilities, and regulatory gaps — directly relevant to understanding DeFi's regulatory landscape.

papers.title: Tokenisation in the Context of Money and Other Assets: Concepts and Implications for Central Banks
papers.description: BIS analysis of tokenisation as a transformative technology for financial markets, covering programmable money, unified ledger concepts, and the implications for monetary policy transmission.

papers.title: Basel III: Prudential Treatment of Cryptoasset Exposures
papers.description: Basel Committee's final standard for the prudential treatment of cryptoasset exposures at banks, including capital requirements for stablecoins — establishing the institutional risk framework.

papers.title: Treasury Management and Cash Optimization in the Digital Age
papers.description: AFP's research on how treasury departments are adopting digital tools — automated cash positioning, real-time payments, and blockchain settlement — to improve working capital efficiency.

papers.title: Instant Payments, Cross-Border — Opportunities and Challenges
papers.description: ECB analysis of the opportunities and challenges in extending instant payments to the cross-border context, including interoperability, FX conversion, and compliance requirements.
